channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Laos government pledges justice in mass alcohol poisoning case that has killed 6 tourists
item.description: The Laotian government on Saturday officially acknowledged the mass poisoning that has killed at least six tourists, pledging it would bring perpetrators to justice.
item.dc:creator: Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Key UN committee adopts resolution paving the way for a first-ever treaty on crimes against humanity
item.description: A key U.N. General Assembly committee has adopted a resolution paving the way for negotiations on a first-ever treaty on preventing and punishing crimes against humanity after Russia dropped amendments that would have derailed the effort.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: As fast fashion's waste pollutes Africa's environment, designers in Ghana are finding a solution
item.description: ACCRA, Ghana (AP) — In a sprawling secondhand clothing market in Ghana’s capital, early morning shoppers jostle as they search through piles of garments, eager to pluck a bargain or a designer find from the stalls selling used and low-quality apparel imported from the West.
item.dc:creator: FRANCIS KOKUTSE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Prominent figure from Canada's trucker protests against COVID-19 restrictions found guilty
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — One of the most prominent figures from Canada's trucker protests against COVID-19 restrictions in 2022 has been found guilty on five counts including mischief and disobeying a court order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Police blow up suspicious package outside US Embassy in London
item.description: Law enforcement conducted a “controlled explosion” of a suspicious package outside of the U.S. Embassy in London on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: House members call for classified briefing on Turkey's ties to Hamas
item.description: More than three dozen Democratic and Republican lawmakers are calling on the Biden administration to offer a classified briefing on Turkey’s support for Hamas, raising concerns that the U.S.-designated terrorist group may relocate its headquarters to the NATO-allied country.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Second Australian teen dies in tainted alcohol case in Laos that has killed 6 tourists
item.description: VANG VIENG, Laos (AP) — A second Australian teenager who fell critically ill after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos has died in a hospital in Bangkok, her family said Friday, bringing the death toll in the mass poisoning of foreign tourists to six.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A Norwegian student has been arrested on charges of spying on the US for Russia
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A Norwegian student in his 20s was arrested on suspicion of spying for Russia and Iran while working as a guard at the U.S. Embassy in Oslo, authorities in Norway have said. The man, who has not been identified, was ordered to be held in custody for four weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German ex-leader Merkel says she felt sorrow at Trump's comeback and recalls awkward non-handshake
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel says she felt “sorrow” at Donald Trump's return to power and recalls that every meeting with him was “a competition: you or me.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani city mourns 42 Shiite Muslims who were ambushed and killed in a gun attack
item.description: Protesters in Pakistan’s restive northwest chanted anti-government slogans and tensions flared as funeral prayers were held for 42 Shiite Muslims who were ambushed and killed by gunmen a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: Riaz Khan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea says Russia supplied air defense missiles to North Korea in return for its troops
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Russia has supplied air defense missile systems to North Korea in exchange for sending its troops to support Russia's war efforts against Ukraine, a top South Korean official said Friday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trudeau to cut sales tax and send checks to millions of Canadians as election looms
item.description: HALIFAX, Nova Scotia (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau 's government announced plans Thursday to temporarily lift the federal sales tax off a number of items and send checks to millions of Canadians who are dealing with rising costs and as a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korean leader says past diplomacy only confirmed US hostility
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said his past negotiations with the United States only confirmed Washington’s “unchangeable” hostility toward his country and described his nuclear buildup as the only way to counter external threats, state media said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN nuclear agency’s board condemns Iran for the 2nd time this year for failing to fully cooperate
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — The U.N. nuclear watchdog’s board on Thursday condemned Iran for failing to cooperate fully with the agency, the second time it has done so in just five months.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Zealand police begin arrests for gang symbol ban as new law takes effect
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A ban on New Zealanders wearing or displaying symbols of gang affiliation in public took effect on Thursday, with police officers making their first arrest for a breach of the law three minutes later.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The world’s top war-crimes court issued arrest warrants Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas’ military chief, accusing them of crimes against humanity in connection with the 13-month war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: ICC issues warrant for Israel's Netanyahu as Gaza death toll soars past 44,000
item.description: The International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants on Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and a Hamas military leader, accusing them of war crimes and crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US briefed Ukraine ahead of Putin's 'experimental Intermediate-range ballistic' attack
item.description: The U.S. alerted Ukraine and other allies ahead of Russia's attack that Moscow might use this "experimental" ballistic weapon to help Kyiv prepare ahead of the attack on Dnipro, a U.S. official confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin says Russia fired intermediate-range ballistic missile at Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Thursday that Russia fired a new, nonnuclear intermediate-range ballistic missile at Ukraine, part of a wide-ranging TV address that sharply criticized the U.S. for escalating the war.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Shares in India's Adani Group plunge 20% after US bribery, fraud indictments
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — One of Asia’s richest men, Indian tycoon Gautam Adani, is again in the spotlight. His companies’ stocks plunged up to 20% in value Thursday after he was indicted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: ASHOK SHARMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US citizen among 4 dead in Laos after suspected alcohol poisoning
item.description: Four people have died after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos after officials said that multiple people had been sickened in town popular with backpackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury imposes sanctions on Russian banks, financial officials
item.description: Russia’s third-largest bank, Gazprombank, and its subsidiaries were hit with U.S. sanctions Thursday, making it the largest bank not already targeted as the Russia-Ukraine war nears the three-year mark.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: International Criminal Court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu, Hamas leader
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has issued arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Mohammed Diab Ibrahim Al-Masri, a Hamas leader commonly known as Mohammed Deif who is presumed dead, according to separate press releases.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Iran hiding missile, drone programs under guise of commercial front to evade sanctions
item.description: Iran turns to commercial sector to conceal its development of its ballistic missile program and evade sanctions as tensions with the West mount amid wars in Ukraine and Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 19 Senators back limiting offensive weapons to Israel in key vote
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday failed to pass three resolutions that would have limited offensive arms to Israel, but 19 senators supported the initiative, showing there is growing resistance to the Israeli war against Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Archaeologists discover 12,000-year-old pebbles that could provide new insights about the wheel
item.description: Researchers gave a detailed look into perforated stones collected out of excavations in Israel, and came to the conclusion that they could have served as spindle whorls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis rejects tradition, opts for humble wooden casket and burial outside Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis announced this week that he intends on having a much more simple funeral than those of his predecessors at the Vatican, including a humble wooden casket.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After 97 UN aid trucks in Gaza are ‘violently looted’ by armed men, food prices soar
item.description: Food prices are rising in Gaza following the robbery of a U.N. convoy over the weekend, during which, drivers were forced to unload aid at gunpoint, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Embassy in Kyiv closed as 'potential significant air attack' looms
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv, Ukraine, has released a warning stating that they have "specific information of a potential significant air attack" taking place on Wednesday, Nov. 20.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sweden, Finland and Norway release new advice on surviving war amid concerns over Russia, Ukraine escalation
item.description: Sweden, Finland and Norway this week released detailed guidance for citizens on how to survive a crisis situation like extreme weather or war-like conditions that could hamper food or internet access.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine fires several US-made longer-range missiles into Russia for the first time
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine fired several American-supplied longer-range missiles into Russia, officials said Tuesday, marking the first time Kyiv used the weapons that way in 1,000 days of war.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: World's hottest calendar drops 2025 edition, proceeds going to Hurricane Helene, Milton efforts
item.description: This year, the funds collected from calendar sales will support those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, to provide immediate relief and aid to impacted communities.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly trying to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on flight
item.description: A South Korean man was arrested in Peru for allegedly attempting to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on a flight back to Korea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC
